programs.expected_behavior: Echoes stdin to stdout (cat program)

programs.name: Brainfuck Nested Loops
programs.expected_behavior: Computes 5*5*2=50 and outputs character with ASCII code 50 ('2')

programs.expected_behavior: Prints 'Hello, World!' by pushing characters onto the stack in reverse and printing them

programs.expected_behavior: Prints its own source code (quine)

programs.name: Whitespace Hello World
programs.expected_behavior: Prints 'Hello World' using only whitespace characters

programs.name: INTERCAL Hello World
programs.source:
DO ,1 <- #13
PLEASE DO ,1 SUB #1 <- #238
DO ,1 SUB #2 <- #112
DO ,1 SUB #3 <- #112
DO ,1 SUB #4 <- #0
DO ,1 SUB #5 <- #64
DO ,1 SUB #6 <- #64
DO ,1 SUB #7 <- #48
DO ,1 SUB #8 <- #26
DO ,1 SUB #9 <- #244
PLEASE DO ,1 SUB #10 <- #168
DO ,1 SUB #11 <- #24
DO ,1 SUB #12 <- #16
DO ,1 SUB #13 <- #214
PLEASE READ OUT ,1
PLEASE GIVE UP
programs.expected_behavior: Prints 'Hello, World!' using INTERCAL's bizarre output encoding

programs.name: Shakespeare Hello World
programs.source:
The Infamous Hello World Program.

Romeo, a young man with a remarkable patience.
Juliet, a likewise young woman of remarkable grace.
Hamlet, a temporary variable.

                    Act I: Hello World!

                    Scene I: Letters.

[Enter Romeo and Juliet]

Romeo:
You pretty little warm thing!

                    Scene II: Output.

Hamlet:
Speak your mind!

[Exeunt]
programs.expected_behavior: Outputs characters using Shakespearean dialogue

programs.name: GolfScript Hello World
programs.expected_behavior: Pushes the string 'Hello World!' onto the stack and outputs it

programs.name: GolfScript Factorial
programs.expected_behavior: Computes factorial of input number

programs.name: Malbolge Hello World
programs.expected_behavior: Prints 'Hello World!' — one of the hardest programs to write

programs.name: Piet Hello World
programs.source:
# Piet is a visual language using bitmap images
# This represents a program that prints Hello World
# Actual Piet programs are images with colored blocks
# Codel size: 1px, using standard palette
# The program pushes H(72), e(101), l(108), l(108), o(111)
# then outputs each character
programs.expected_behavior: Prints 'Hello World' — program is encoded as a colored image

programs.name: Jelly Hello World
programs.expected_behavior: Prints 'Hello World' using Jelly's string compression

programs.name: APL Hello World
programs.expected_behavior: Prints 'Hello World'

programs.name: Befunge Calculator
programs.expected_behavior: Reads two numbers and performs arithmetic based on direction

programs.name: Brainfuck Adder
programs.expected_behavior: Reads two single-digit numbers and outputs their sum

programs.name: FALSE Fibonacci
programs.expected_behavior: Prints Fibonacci numbers

programs.name: Chef Hello World
programs.source:
Hello World Souffle.

Ingredients.
72 g haricot beans
101 g butter
108 g eggs
108 g sugar
111 g flour
32 g olive oil
87 g water
111 g milk
114 g zucchini
100 g salmon
33 g chocolate

Method.
Put haricot beans into mixing bowl.
Put butter into mixing bowl.
Put eggs into mixing bowl.
Put sugar into mixing bowl.
Put flour into mixing bowl.
Put olive oil into mixing bowl.
Put water into mixing bowl.
Put milk into mixing bowl.
Put zucchini into mixing bowl.
Put salmon into mixing bowl.
Put chocolate into mixing bowl.
Pour contents of mixing bowl into baking dish.

Serves 1.
programs.expected_behavior: Prints 'Hello World!' using ASCII values of ingredient amounts

programs.name: Whitespace Counter
programs.expected_behavior: Pushes numbers and prints them — a simple counter using only whitespace

programs.name: Brainfuck Quine
programs.expected_behavior: A quine — outputs its own source code

programs.name: INTERCAL Calculator
programs.source:
DO WRITE IN .1
DO WRITE IN .2
PLEASE DO .3 <- .1 "$.2
PLEASE DO READ OUT .3
PLEASE GIVE UP
programs.expected_behavior: Reads two numbers and outputs their sum using INTERCAL's mingle/select operations

programs.name: Piet Fibonacci Fragment
programs.source:
# Piet Fibonacci program
# Pushes 1, 1, then repeatedly:
# Duplicate top, rotate, add, duplicate, output number
# Codel sequence: push(1) push(1) [dup roll add dup out_num]
programs.expected_behavior: Generates Fibonacci sequence — encoded as colored image blocks
